app_details.name: Wanger Family Hub
app_details.core_goal: Provide a fast, app-like mobile experience (PWA) with persistent bottom navigation, personalized user states, and robust iOS/Android safe area handling.

theme_and_archetype.primary_archetype: Archetype 4 (Swiss & High-Contrast) blended with Archetype 3 (Soft Utility)
theme_and_archetype.reasoning: A utility PWA needs high clarity and functional structure (Swiss/High-Contrast) but should feel friendly and approachable for daily family use. We use neutral Structural Greys to let the user-specific vibrant colors (Tim's Blue, Iris's Rose) stand out as the primary visual identifiers.
theme_and_archetype.theme_switch: Must support system preference auto-detect AND manual toggle via a switch/icon in the top bar.

layout_and_spacing.container: w-full max-w-[412px] mx-auto min-h-[100dvh] relative overflow-hidden bg-background shadow-2xl shadow-black/10 flex flex-col

safe_area_handling.meta_tag: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, viewport-fit=cover, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=0">
safe_area_handling.css_variables: Must use env(safe-area-inset-bottom) for bottom nav padding and env(safe-area-inset-top) for top bar padding.

layout_and_spacing.touch_targets: All interactive elements (buttons, nav items) MUST have a minimum height and width of 48px to exceed the 44px HIG requirement.
layout_and_spacing.interactions: NO hover-only interactions. Use active states (active:scale-95, active:opacity-70) for touch feedback.

top_bar.layout: Fixed top, w-full max-w-[412px] z-50 flex items-center justify-between px-4 h-16 pt-[env(safe-area-inset-top)] border-b
top_bar.elements: Left: User Avatar (circle, colored based on logged-in user) + Display Name. Right: Dark Mode Toggle (Sun/Moon icon).
top_bar.avatar: w-8 h-8 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center text-white font-bold text-sm

bottom_nav.layout: Fixed bottom, w-full max-w-[412px] z-50 flex items-center justify-around border-t
bottom_nav.item_styles: flex flex-col items-center justify-center w-[20%] min-h-[64px] transition-colors
bottom_nav.active_state: Color should reflect the current logged-in user's color (Tim=Blue, Iris=Rose). Inactive=text-slate-400.

empty_states.layout: flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full text-center space-y-4 py-12
empty_states.elements: 1. Large light Lucide icon (w-16 h-16 stroke-1 text-slate-300 dark:text-slate-700). 2. Headline (h2). 3. Subtle description. 4. Optional CTA button colored with current user's theme.

login_page.layout: flex flex-col items-center justify-center min-h-[100dvh] p-6 space-y-8
login_page.form: 2 inputs (Email, Password), 1 primary CTA button. Native styling, large inputs (h-14).

motion.tab_transitions: Use Framer Motion <AnimatePresence mode='wait'>. Very subtle fade and vertical shift (y: 10 -> 0) with a quick spring duration (0.2s) to keep it feeling snappy and native.

assets.icons: Use 'lucide-react' for all UI icons. It is clean, lightweight, and scales well.
assets.fonts_installation: Add Google Fonts imports for 'Manrope' (Headings) and 'DM Sans' (Body) in index.css.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Setup Context: Create an Auth/User Context that stores whether 'Tim' or 'Iris' is logged in, and their associated color theme (#3B82F6 or #F43F5E).
2. Theme Provider: Implement standard Tailwind dark mode with system preference auto-detection + a manual override state.
3. PWA Shell: Ensure index.html has <meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1, viewport-fit=cover'> to enable safe-area css variables.
4. Component Architecture: Create AppShell component that wraps the selected tab content, holding the TopBar and BottomNav.
5. Testing Attributes: Apply data-testid to Login form, BottomNav tabs, Theme toggle, and Logout button.
6. Styling: Ensure the app width is restricted to max-w-[412px] mx-auto on larger screens to simulate a mobile device, with a subtle border/shadow.
7. Mock Authentication: For MVP, a simple hardcoded login checking against 'tim@...' and 'iris@...' or a user selector button on the login screen is acceptable if backend isn't requested.
